predictions.title: AMD discloses AI-accelerator revenue as 10-Q segment footnote by Q3 2026
predictions.body: By Q3 2026 the AMD May 5 Q1 2026 print — Q1 revenue $10.25B (vs $9.85B consensus, +38% YoY), adjusted EPS $1.37 (vs $1.27 consensus, +9.6% beat), Data Center segment $5.78B at +57% YoY (consensus $5.56B / +51.5%), non-GAAP gross margin 55%, Q2 guide $11.2B +/- $300M (vs Street $10.52B, implying +50.6% YoY and +9.1% sequential), CEO Lisa Su declaring Data Center now the "primary driver of revenue and earnings growth," MI450 Series + Helios customer engagement "strengthening," AMD shares +4.4% after-hours — combined with the Apr 29 MSFT $37B AI-run-rate single-line-item precedent, the May 4 Anthropic + OpenAI parallel JV launches that monetize frontier-AI as a structured-equity wrapper, the May 5 Anthropic 10-agent financial-services launch with native Microsoft 365 + Moody's MCP integration, and the standing $700B 2026 Big-Tech AI-capex tape forces AMD (or a NVIDIA / Broadcom sibling) to publish AI-accelerator-only revenue as a 10-Q segment footnote ahead of any SEC mandate. The disclosure precedent is most likely to mirror the MSFT $37B AI run-rate footnote shape — a single-line-item with optional supplementary breakout — rather than a more invasive cloud-segment-style disclosure regime. Forcing function is the asymmetric narrative pressure: Lisa Su has now publicly named Data Center the primary growth engine on the print itself, but the segment still bundles EPYC server CPUs with Instinct MI300/MI325 AI accelerators; buyside models cannot triangulate accelerator-only ASP and unit economics from the bundled line, and the May 5 Anthropic-finance-agent + FIS-AML stack makes the AI-accelerator-only sub-line the operative buyside disclosure ask. AMD CFO commentary on the next earnings call is the lead indicator; NVIDIA + Broadcom follow within one or two filing cycles via competitive-disclosure pressure.

reasoning.because: AMD Q1 print landed +38% YoY $10.25B with Data Center +57% YoY at $5.78B, named primary driver by Lisa Su.
reasoning.given: MSFT $37B AI run-rate single-line precedent + parallel May 4 AI-JV equity wrappers + $700B 2026 capex tape harden the buyside disclosure bar.
reasoning.so_that: AMD publishes AI-accelerator-only revenue as a 10-Q segment footnote (MI300/MI325/MI450 split from EPYC) ahead of any SEC mandate.
reasoning.landing: By Q3 2026; AMD most-likely first mover, NVIDIA + Broadcom follow within one or two filing cycles.
reasoning.plain_language: AMD's data center sales jumped 57 percent and Lisa Su called it the main growth engine, so investors will want AI chip revenue shown separately.

predictions.summary: AMD's May 5 Q1 print beat ($10.25B, +38% YoY; Data Center $5.78B, +57% YoY) with Lisa Su naming Data Center the primary growth driver and Q2 guide $11.2B. Combined with the MSFT $37B AI run-rate precedent, by Q3 2026 AMD is expected to publish AI-accelerator-only revenue as a 10-Q segment footnote.

predictions.title: NIST publishes non-human-identity control profile by Q4 2026
predictions.body: Cisco's May 4 $400M Astrix Security acquisition makes non-human-identity (NHI) governance an explicit M&A-tier primitive on the same week as Microsoft Agent 365 GA (May 1, $15/user/mo M365 E7 SKU with per-agent Defender relationship map + Entra ID + Purview + Intune) and the May 1 Cloud Security Alliance posting that names identity as Zero Trust's first pillar in the agentic-AI era. The structural backdrop is severe: ~7,000 internet-exposed MCP servers as of early 2026 with roughly half operating with no authorization controls, AI agents and other non-human identities now outnumber human users ~100:1 in major enterprises (per Cisco's customer survey), and MCP itself ships with no built-in identity, no least-privilege enforcement, and no audit trail. Cisco's planned integration of Astrix into Cisco Identity Intelligence + Secure Access + Duo IAM converges with Microsoft's per-agent identity model into a de-facto two-vendor NHI control-plane primitive — but the procurement tier above (federal CISO offices, banking regulators, healthcare HIPAA-scoped buyers) requires a published-standard reference, not a vendor-specific architecture diagram. By Q4 2026 NIST is expected to publish a dedicated NHI control profile (most likely as a SP 800-207-series Zero Trust supplement or an SP 800-53 control-family overlay) treating machine identities, agent identities, and service-account identities as a distinct compliance category with discovery, lifecycle, least-privilege, and audit-trail control families. CISA agentic-AI guidance and SEC cyber-disclosure-rule commentary follow within one quarter via the same forcing function — the $400M Astrix M&A price tag is itself a regulator-readable signal that the category has crossed into balance-sheet materiality.

reasoning.because: Cisco $400M Astrix M&A + Microsoft Agent 365 GA + CSA first-pillar framing converge in one week on NHI as primitive.
reasoning.given: ~7,000 exposed MCP servers (half unauthenticated) + 100:1 NHI-to-human ratio leave the federal/banking buyer tier without a published standard.
reasoning.so_that: NIST publishes a dedicated NHI control profile (SP 800-207 supplement or SP 800-53 overlay) naming machine + agent + service-account identities a distinct compliance category.
reasoning.landing: By Q4 2026; CISA + SEC commentary follow within one quarter.
reasoning.plain_language: AI agents outnumber humans 100-to-1 inside companies and most have no security check — the US standards body will soon name them their own category.

predictions.summary: Cisco $400M Astrix + Microsoft Agent 365 GA + CSA first-pillar framing land with ~7,000 exposed MCP servers (half unauthenticated) and a 100:1 NHI ratio. By Q4 2026 NIST is expected to publish a control profile naming machine, agent, and service-account identities a distinct compliance category.

predictions.title: OCC publishes agentic-AI Model Risk Management addendum by Q3 2026
predictions.body: By Q3 2026 the May 5 Anthropic 10-agent financial-services launch — pitch builder, meeting preparer, earnings reviewer, model builder, market researcher, valuation reviewer, general ledger reconciler, month-end closer, statement auditor, and KYC screener running on Claude Opus 4.7 (state-of-the-art 64.37% on Vals AI Finance Agent benchmark), Microsoft 365 add-ins spanning Excel + PowerPoint + Word with Outlook coming, native MCP connectors for Dun & Bradstreet, Fiscal AI, Financial Modeling Prep, Guidepoint, IBISWorld, SS&C IntraLinks, Third Bridge, Verisk, the Moody's MCP app delivering credit ratings + data on 600M+ public/private companies natively into Claude, and a testimonial roster including Citadel, BNY, Carlyle, Mizuho, Travelers, Walleye Capital — combined with the May 4 BusinessWire FIS + Anthropic AML Financial Crimes AI Agent (FIS powers ~12% of the global economy; auto-assembles evidence across a bank's core systems, evaluates against known typologies, surfaces highest-risk cases, drafts SAR narratives; BMO + Amalgamated Bank are first deployment customers; broader availability targeted H2 2026) forces ≥1 banking regulator (OCC most likely, FRB / FCA / EBA siblings) to publish an agentic-AI use-of-AI-in-banking guidance OR a Model Risk Management addendum that explicitly names agent-based decisioning and sets audit-trail / human-in-the-loop / SAR-narrative-attribution requirements. The forcing function is dual: SR 11-7 / OCC 2011-12 was written for static models, but agentic systems compose tools, call external data brokers via MCP, and chain decisions across multiple core systems in minutes — examiners cannot apply the legacy framework cleanly to a Claude-driven AML pipeline that auto-assembles SAR evidence and drafts the narrative. The OCC is the most-likely first mover because BMO is OCC-supervised at the US holdco level and the May 4 FIS announcement names a regulatory-grade audit trail as a feature; the OCC's existing model-risk leadership (SR 11-7 was Fed-OCC joint) makes an addendum the cleanest delivery shape. FRB SR-letter or FCA Dear-CEO letter follows within one filing cycle.

reasoning.because: Anthropic 10-agent finance launch + FIS-Anthropic AML agent on May 4-5 land Tier-1 banking workloads on agentic AI in one news cycle.
reasoning.given: SR 11-7 / OCC 2011-12 model-risk framework was written for static models, not agents that chain tools + MCP data brokers across core systems.
reasoning.so_that: OCC publishes an agentic-AI use-of-AI-in-banking guidance or Model Risk Management addendum naming agent-based decisioning + audit-trail rules.
reasoning.landing: By Q3 2026; OCC most-likely first mover via BMO supervision linkage, FRB / FCA / EBA siblings within one cycle.
reasoning.plain_language: Banks just put AI agents on AML and core finance work, so a US bank regulator will publish rules that name AI agents specifically.

predictions.summary: Anthropic's May 5 10-agent finance launch on Claude Opus 4.7 with M365 + Moody's MCP, plus the May 4 FIS-Anthropic AML Financial Crimes Agent at BMO + Amalgamated, lands agentic AI inside Tier-1 banking. By Q3 2026 a regulator (OCC likely) publishes an agentic-AI Model Risk addendum.
